zh.Middlewich, Cheshire, England: 英國英格蘭爾米德列維其
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Toft於1231年出生在英國爾阿爾德福德時，她的父親Roger de Toft31歲，母親Beatrix de Venables-1歲。她於1261年之前與William Mainwaring結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1308年享年77歲。

nameMeanings.name: Toft
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Hjalmer, Nels, Thorval.

English (Lancashire, Cheshire and Staffordshire): habitational name from any of various places, for example in Cambridgeshire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, and Warwickshire, named with Middle English toft(e) (Old Norse topt, Old Danish toft), denoting a plot of land on which a house stands or formerly stood. The surname may also be topographic, for someone who lived by or on a toft.
Scandinavian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads or placenames derived from Old Norse topt ‘curtilage, site, homestead’.

variants.name: Tufts
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of Tofts, itself a variant of Toft with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Tuft
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads so named, from Old Norse tupt ‘site, lot’.
Possibly an altered form of South German Duft, a topographic name meaning ‘swamp, moor’.

variants.name: Taft
meanings: English (mainly Staffordshire and Derbyshire): variant of   Toft  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial or metonymic occupational name from German Taft ‘taffeta’.

History: Robert Taft (born c. 1640), lived in Braintree, MA, and subsequently Mendon, MA. — Alphonso Taft (1810–91), jurist and politician born in Townshend, VT, was the father of William Howard Taft (1857–1930), 27th president of the US and chief justice of the US Supreme Court.

variants.name: Tofte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Hjalmer, Iver, Steinar, Thora.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Toft was born in 1231, in Aldford, Cheshire, England, her father, Roger de Toft, was 31 and her mother, Beatrix de Venables, was -1. She married William Mainwaring before 1261. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1308, at the age of 77.
